meta.title: Chihuahuas for Grandma
meta.subtitle: Dogs we picked out — tap one for the full story.

targetWeightLbs.note: Goal is ~5–6 lbs; always confirm with the rescue.

dogs.name: Tiki
dogs.postedAt: ~1 month ago on Adopt a Pet (verify on site)
dogs.listingUpdatedAt: ~1 month ago on Adopt a Pet
dogs.personality: Listing notes: good with kids and dogs. Purebred. Personality tags not filled on Adopt a Pet.
dogs.summary: Young chi puppy — fee $400, weight not listed on page.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (Rescue Fenix): Rescue Fenix, is a not for profit 501(c)3. We are an all volunteer all breed rescue mobilized by the feeling of compassion for animal welfare: those animals who are in danger, sick, abused and neglect. We ensure that each animal rescued, receives medical care and rehabilitation prior to rehoming them into a suitable furever home. We do not receive government funding and rely solely on the generosity of our donors.
dogs.health: Shots current per listing. Spay/neuter not listed under “My health” for this puppy — confirm with rescue.
dogs.details: Details on listing: Good with kids, good with dogs, purebred.
dogs.adoptionProcess: Rescue states: “Our goal is to make the perfect match!” Full steps on their Adopt a Pet page and rescue site.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: Short blurb on site only — expect application and rescue approval like other Fenix dogs; confirm on the live Adopt a Pet listing.
dogs.fitAnalysis: Puppy energy and unknown weight vs. your 5–6 lb lap-dog goal — worth asking weight and temperament directly. Good with kids/dogs per listing.

dogs.adoptionProcess: “Our goal is to make the perfect match!” — see listing and rescue site for application.

dogs.fitAnalysis: Very light at 2 lbs (below your target range but still growing). Puppy = energy and training. Confirm expected adult size with rescue.

dogs.name: Tula
dogs.postedAt: ~3 days ago on Adopt a Pet
dogs.personality: Listing traits: Affectionate, Chill, Curious, Gentle, Patient, Quiet, Snuggly. Good with kids and dogs; housetrained.
dogs.summary: Young adult chi — snuggly/chill tags, $500, weight not on page.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (Dulce Vida Animal Rescue): At Dulce Vida Animal Rescue, we are driven by compassion and a commitment to animal welfare. Our mission is to rescue, rehabilitate, and find loving forever homes for sick, abused, and abandoned dogs and cats. We work to address the root causes of animal suffering by partnering with local clinics to provide spay and neuter services and engaging with the community to promote responsible pet ownership.
dogs.details: Housetrained per listing. Apply online button on Adopt a Pet.
dogs.adoptionProcess: Listing shows adoption process as “N/A” in the expandable — follow “Apply Online” and email adoptions@dulcevidarescue.org.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: Fee $500. Site had minimal process text; use Apply Online and contact the rescue for steps.
dogs.fitAnalysis: Strong lap-dog signals from traits (Snuggly, Quiet, Chill). Weight missing — ask rescue. Good match to explore if she stays small.

dogs.name: Milo
dogs.color: Not listed on Adopt a Pet
dogs.postedAt: Posted 2+ months ago on Adopt a Pet
dogs.adoptionFee: Not listed on page — ask rescue
dogs.personality: Purebred per listing; no personality word list on snippet.
dogs.summary: Senior small chi, 3 lbs — adult lap size, fee not shown on page.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (Wet Noses Rescue): Two women, one American, and one Mexican met in Sayulita through their mutual concern for the many sick, abandoned, neglected, and abused dogs and cats in the area. They began working together informally to rescue, rehabilitate, and find homes for these animals. Their efforts eventually led to the formation of Wet Noses Rescue, which has grown from a small backyard operation to an expanding nonprofit housed on a property with dedicated facilities in Punta de Mita. Providing the animals with health care, rehabilitation, socialization, and careful evaluation which is needed for a successful adoption to new homes in the United States and Canada. Wet Noses Rescue also provides Spay/Neuter clinics in the local community to reduce overpopulation and vaccinations to reduce the prevalence of serious diseases such as Parvo and Distemper.
dogs.details: Purebred. Listing adoption steps: Submit application → Interview → Home check.
dogs.adoptionProcess: From Adopt a Pet listing: We have been refining the process for a few years now. It is important to us that the pet matches the new owner as much as possible. (See listing for any expanded steps.)
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: Application, interview, and home check per listing. Adoption fee not shown on listing — confirm with Wet Noses Rescue.
dogs.fitAnalysis: 3 lbs is under your 5–6 lb target but very portable; older dog may be calmer — ask about temperament and handling.

dogs.name: Latte
dogs.caretakerName: Chihuahua Rescue of San Diego County
dogs.personality: Mix; no trait list in snippet — dogs live in private foster homes per rescue blurb.
dogs.summary: 6 lb mix — right in target weight, $350.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (Chihuahua Rescue of San Diego County): We are a rescue consisting of an all volunteer organization where all of our dogs are in private homes everyday. We do not have a shelter and our dogs are not kept in cages. Our dogs are from county animals shelters or from previous owners that are no longer able to care for their dog. All of our dogs are started on housebreaking, however, we do not guarantee that they will be housebroken. We offer education on how to housebreak a dog as well as other information on different situations. Every dog is spayed or neutered, current on shots, dewormed, frontline for fleas and ticks. Thank you for considering a rescue dog!!!
dogs.adoptionProcess: Application at http://rescueachi.com/adoption-application/ → approval → meet dog (often via foster) → possible home visit → contract and fee. Landlord contact if you rent.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: Apply online, get approved, meet in person (often through foster), possible home inspection, then contract and $350 fee.
dogs.fitAnalysis: 6 lbs matches your goal band. Terrier mix may add spark — meet him and ask foster about lap/cuddle style.

dogs.adoptionProcess: Same as Latte: application, approval, meet, possible home visit, contract, fee.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: $400 fee; apply at rescueachi.com/adoption-application/ then follow their foster meet process.
dogs.fitAnalysis: Lighter than 5–6 lb goal but adult — may be calmer; confirm personality with foster.

dogs.name: Jelly
dogs.personality: Good with kids and dogs per listing.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (Second Chance Dog Rescue): Second Chance Dog Rescue was formed to fulfill a desperate need in the community to Rescue, Rehabilitate & Re-home homeless dogs from the over-crowded local shelters, and from families who can no longer take care of their dogs (on a case-by-case basis only). Our program is based on foster care, and all rescued dogs spend their first days at a loving foster home where they receive personalized care. As part of our program, we ensure that all dogs are vaccinated, are spayed or neutered, and have been socialized by their foster family. In addition, Second Chance Dog Rescue provides support for potential new owners and monitors progress for both new pet parents and their adopted dogs. This helps guarantee that our dogs go to good, loving forever homes.
dogs.details: Second Chance: questionnaire before meet, contract, fees, 2-week trial; puppy may need training deposit per listing.
dogs.adoptionProcess: Complete Adoption Questionnaire (online or in person) before meeting dogs → Adoption Contract and fees → 2-week trial. Puppy adoptions may require refundable training deposit with proof of training completion.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: Questionnaire first, then contract and fee; two-week trial. Possible puppy training deposit — read listing carefully.
dogs.fitAnalysis: Puppy + terrier mix + unknown weight = more uncertainty for a strict small lap-dog profile; still worth asking size expectations.

dogs.adoptionProcess: Rescue Fenix: “Our goal is to make the perfect match!”

dogs.fitAnalysis: Very small now; puppy behavior. Aligns on breed but not on age/energy vs. quiet lap life yet.

dogs.name: Magnolia
dogs.postedAt: ~1 day ago on Adopt a Pet
dogs.personality: Affectionate, Curious, Gentle, Playful, Snuggly. Good with kids and dogs.
dogs.summary: 4 lb puppy girl — traits include Snuggly, $495.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (ALMA Rescue 501c3): ALMA Rescue is a So. Cal 501c3 who rescues dogs from throughout Mexico and provides them with all of the veterinary care they need. We provide ALL vaccinations, de-worming, flea/tick prevention, micro-chipping & spay and neuter surgery. We operate two "rescue homes:" one in Baja and one in Tijuana and our rescued pups have full time caretakers to ensure they get the best of care. We also put on multiple large FREE Spay and Neuter Clinics 2-3 times per year in both areas to help with the serious and heartbreaking pet overpopulation problem there. To date, we have fixed over 2500 animals! ALMA was founded by childhood friends Erin Riley-Carrasco and Suzie Williamson. ALMA Rescue has a dedicated group of wonderful volunteers who help transport and foster all of our rescued dogs. We are passionate about helping to stop animal suffering and encourage everyone to save a life and ADOPT DON'T SHOP!
dogs.details: If you rent, listing says landlord letter required for breed approval.
dogs.adoptionProcess: Renters need landlord letter that dogs are allowed and breed is approved. Otherwise follow ALMA’s application flow on their site.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: $495; snuggly/playful puppy. Landlord paperwork if renting — see listing.
dogs.fitAnalysis: Snuggly + gentle tags fit Grandma goals; still a puppy. 4 lbs now — ask expected adult weight.

Cal 501c3 who rescues dogs from throughout Mexico and provides them with all of the veterinary care they need. We provide ALL vaccinations, de-worming, flea/tick prevention, micro-chipping & spay and neuter surgery. We also put on multiple large FREE Spay and Neuter Clinics 2-3 times per year in both areas to help with the serious and heartbreaking pet overpopulation problem there. To date, we have fixed over 2500 animals! ALMA was founded by childhood friends Erin Riley-Carrasco and Suzie Williamson. ALMA Rescue has a dedicated group of wonderful volunteers who help transport and foster all of our rescued dogs. We are passionate about helping to stop animal suffering and encourage everyone to save a life and ADOPT DON'T SHOP!
dogs.details: Landlord letter if renting (same ALMA policy).
dogs.adoptionProcess: Same as Magnolia — landlord letter for renters; contact ALMA.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: $495; snuggly puppy from ALMA. Confirm with rescue you are not double-booking meet times.
dogs.fitAnalysis: Same snuggly puppy profile as Magnolia — pick one or ask rescue which pup fits your home best.

dogs.name: Corazón
dogs.personality: Good with kids, dogs, cats; housetrained per listing.
dogs.summary: 10 lbs — above your small target; cat-friendly + housetrained.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (4theloveofdogs / Yuribia): I'm Yuribia, I rescue dogs from terrible situations from the streets of Tijuana and San Diego and Los Angeles. It hurts my soul to see these beautiful animals suffer from neglect, illness and abuse. I love animals. My mission is to rescue these sweet souls, and get them the medical attention they require to see them heal, grow and thrive so that they can be adopted into a loving home that will give them the care that they deserve. I rescue the dogs that have been abandoned, abused, neglected and forgotten, some of them have special needs, and are blind, deaf or have had tumors and growths, and skin conditions or broken bones. I make sure that these dogs are healthy and ready to start a new, happy life with you. Contact: 4theloveofdogsoceanside@gmail.com
dogs.details: Larger than typical 5–6 lb goal; good if you want sturdier small dog.
dogs.adoptionProcess: Applications reviewed in order; they reply or call with questions to ensure fit.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: Email/contact through Adopt a Pet; informal process per listing — ask fee and steps.
dogs.howToApply: Ask About Me on Adopt a Pet / email rescuer
dogs.fitAnalysis: 10 lbs is well above your target band; positives: housetrained, good with cats. Best if Grandma wants a slightly bigger chi.

dogs.name: Roger
dogs.postedAt: ~1 week ago on Adopt a Pet (verify on site)
dogs.adoptionFee: Not shown in listing text we synced — confirm on Adopt a Pet
dogs.personality: Good with dogs; purebred (per Adopt a Pet “My details”).
dogs.summary: Young male Chihuahua — weight not listed on Adopt a Pet; Second Chance Dog Rescue.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (Second Chance Dog Rescue): Second Chance Dog Rescue was formed to fulfill a desperate need in the community to Rescue, Rehabilitate & Re-home homeless dogs from the over-crowded local shelters, and from families who can no longer take care of their dogs (on a case-by-case basis only). Our program is based on foster care, and all rescued dogs spend their first days at a loving foster home where they receive personalized care. As part of our program, we ensure that all dogs are vaccinated, are spayed or neutered, and have been socialized by their foster family. In addition, Second Chance Dog Rescue provides support for potential new owners and monitors progress for both new pet parents and their adopted dogs. This helps guarantee that our dogs go to good, loving forever homes.
dogs.health: Shots current; spayed/neutered per Adopt a Pet “My health.”
dogs.details: Purebred; good with dogs. Email info@secondchancedogrescue.org · http://www.SecondChanceDogRescue.org
dogs.adoptionProcess: We require an Adoption Questionnaire to be completed - whether online or in person - before meeting any of our dogs. Upon approval of the Questionnaire, a potential adopter will be required to complete an Adoption Contract and pay adoption fees. All of our adoptions start with the first 2 weeks as a 'trial' period, to ensure the adoption is a good fit. Adoption of a puppy may require a refundable training deposit, which will be refunded upon completion of training with a Certfied dog trainer, and submission of certificate of completion to our office.
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: Questionnaire before meets; contract and fees; 2-week trial; possible puppy training deposit per listing.
dogs.fitAnalysis: Weight not listed on Adopt a Pet — ask rescue about size vs. your 5–6 lb goal.

When the live site shows “Looks like this pet may have been adopted!”, it does not display age, sex, weight, or rescue story until the pet is listed again. Bookmark the listing link and refresh periodically, or search the rescue on Adopt a Pet for Dylan by name.

dogs.summary: 9 lb adult — above your 5–6 lb band; strong snuggly tags, Road To Freedom Rescue.
dogs.myStory: From Adopt a Pet — “More about this rescue” (Road To Freedom Animal Rescue): Road To Freedom Animal Rescue is a volunteer group of dedicated women who strive to bring brighter futures to rescue dogs. Our mission is directed at saving as many animal lives from horrific conditions such as homelessness and neglect. We do this by providing compassion, quality lifesaving veterinary care, adoption, and education.
dogs.details: Listed under Road To Freedom Rescue. Email info@roadtofreedomrescue.com · http://roadtofreedomrescue.com/
dogs.adoptionProcess: The first step in our adoption process is to complete an application. (See Adopt a Pet listing for full steps.)
dogs.adoptionProcessSummary: Application first — details on listing and rescue site.
dogs.fitAnalysis: 9 lbs is above your target range; snuggly/gentle traits may still suit if Grandma wants a slightly larger lap dog.
